knowledge.title: Nuke A site (upper): heaven + rafters crossfire
knowledge.description: CT A: one player heaven (above site), one player rafters (off-angle from CT spawn). Heaven covers the entry from outside/squeaky, rafters traps the silo push. Both on site = double frag from a heaven smoke.
knowledge.situation: CT A defense, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: heaven-rafters crossfire is standard Nuke A setup

knowledge.title: Nuke B site (lower): close + ramp anchor
knowledge.description: CT B: one player on B (close angle on doors), one player ramp room (off-angle on the second push). B player traps the entry, ramp player traps the trade. CT spawn rotation is ~6 seconds — fast holds matter.
knowledge.situation: CT B defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B-rampoom crossfire is standard Nuke B setup

knowledge.title: Nuke outside: outside player with AWP
knowledge.description: CT outside player holds outside with AWP from the silo angle. Outside is the highest-value AWP position on Nuke — it covers heaven access AND outside rotates. Reposition after first shot.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP outside
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: outside AWP averages ~1.5 opening kills per round on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke A execute (outside route): smoke heaven + molly silo + flash entry
knowledge.description: A execute via outside: smoke heaven (cuts the high angle), molly silo (clears close hold), flash from outside for entry. This is the canonical Nuke A take — outside route opens 3 entry points.
knowledge.situation: T-side A execute via outside
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: outside A execute is the most reliable Nuke T strat

knowledge.title: Nuke B execute: smoke vent + smoke ladder + molly newbox
knowledge.description: B execute: smoke vent (cuts the rotate from CT), smoke ladder room (cuts trade from above), molly newbox (clears the close hold), flash from main for entry. ~68% plant rate with full B execute on Nuke.
knowledge.situation: T-side B execute, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility B execute is most reliable Nuke B strat

knowledge.title: Nuke ramp control utility: smoke + flash for ramp room
knowledge.description: T ramp control: smoke ramp room (cuts the trade from above), flash for the entry. Ramp control opens 3 site hit options — without ramp, T side is forced into outside-only commits.
knowledge.situation: T ramp control
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ramp control predicts T round win on Nuke better than mid control on most other maps

knowledge.title: Nuke pistol: CT outside stack
knowledge.description: Nuke CT pistol: stack outside with the outside player + 1 secondary pistol player. T side has the worst pistol economy on Nuke — the long sightlines reward kevlar and pre-aim. ~62% CT pistol WR.
knowledge.situation: Nuke CT pistol
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Nuke CT pistol WR is the highest of any active duty map

knowledge.title: Nuke force buy: B through main with utility
knowledge.description: Nuke B forces work because B is utility-driven and the entry is short. A forces have very low success on Nuke — outside is too punishing under-buys.
knowledge.situation: T force buy decision on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Nuke B force wins ~38%, A force <20%

knowledge.title: Nuke save: route through T spawn, never through outside
knowledge.description: Nuke saves: always route back through T spawn. Outside saves are the worst-case scenario — outside CTs catch savers with AWPs from far. Strict T spawn discipline only.
knowledge.situation: Nuke save round
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Nuke save retention drops ~25% if savers route outside

knowledge.title: Nuke outside duel: pre-aim head level on the silo angle
knowledge.description: Outside duels are decided by pre-aim — the silo angle is head-level for both sides. Whoever has the crosshair on the gap wins. Most outside deaths at intermediate skill come from low crosshair.
knowledge.situation: Outside silo AWP duel
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top outside AWPers pre-aim head level >95%

knowledge.title: Nuke heaven push: walk and pre-aim ladder
knowledge.description: Pushing heaven from CT side: walk silently and pre-aim the ladder exit. The T player coming up the ladder has zero info — you have all the time. Slow plays beat aim plays here.
knowledge.situation: CT heaven push from CT spawn
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: walked heaven pushes win ~70% vs ran heaven pushes ~45%

knowledge.title: Nuke ramp duel: spray transfer through smoke
knowledge.description: Ramp duels are close range and often through smoke. Spray transfer (kill one, walk crosshair to next) wins ramp fights more than tap firing.
knowledge.situation: Ramp room duel
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: spray-transfer accounts for ~45% of ramp room frags

knowledge.title: Nuke outside contact: rotate to inside on a trade
knowledge.description: If you take a trade outside, rotate inside (lobby or main) immediately. The outside trade pulls the CT outside player; inside opens up. Slow play after outside contact gives the CT time to re-rotate.
knowledge.situation: Outside contact, deciding next call
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: post-outside-contact inside commit wins ~60% on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke IGL: outside info gates everything
knowledge.description: Outside info on Nuke is the master variable. Whoever wins outside controls round options. Send 1-2 players outside on every default and base your call on outside outcome.
knowledge.situation: T default information gathering
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 Nuke IGLs base ~70% of commits on outside outcome

knowledge.title: Nuke A fake: 25-second commit
knowledge.description: Nuke A fakes commit utility for 25 seconds (heaven smoke, silo molly, gunfire) then re-rotate to B through main. CT will burn rotates. 25-second window is the sweet spot on Nuke.
knowledge.situation: A fake into B execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 25-second A fake to B execute wins ~55% on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke A retake: from heaven with smoke + flash
knowledge.description: A retake from heaven (rotate from CT spawn) with smoke (cuts the lurker), flash, push. Heaven retake is the only viable A retake on Nuke — site retakes from below get traded by the lurker.
knowledge.situation: CT retake A site
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: heaven retakes win ~52% on Nuke A

knowledge.title: Nuke B post-plant: hold from secret or main lurk
knowledge.description: Post-plant B: lurk back through secret (cuts the rotate from CT spawn) or through main (holds the main retake). Both outperform sitting on site by ~12 points.
knowledge.situation: T post-plant B
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: secret/main lurks outperform site-hold by ~12 points on Nuke B

knowledge.title: Nuke A plant: in front of silo, exposed to heaven
knowledge.description: Default A plant is in front of silo, exposed to heaven. Heaven defuse is the worst angle for the CT — your post-plant lurker can pre-fire the defuse from outside.
knowledge.situation: T A plant location
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: front-of-silo plant has highest post-plant WR on Nuke A

knowledge.title: Nuke anti-strat: counter outside rush with garage molly
knowledge.description: If the T keeps rushing outside, molly the garage exit area. This forces the rush to wait or take fire damage. Outside rushes on Nuke depend on momentum — a 7-second molly delay breaks the timing completely.
knowledge.situation: CT defending against outside rushes on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: garage molly reduces outside rush success from ~45% to ~20%

knowledge.title: Nuke anti-strat: counter ramp push with ramp smoke + flash
knowledge.description: If the T keeps taking ramp control, smoke the ramp entrance and flash through the smoke to retake control. Ramp on Nuke is the key mid-round area — losing it gives the T a B option. Fight for ramp control with utility, never dry.
knowledge.situation: CT losing ramp control on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: smoke + flash ramp retake succeeds ~55% on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke entry: ramp entry with flash for trophy
knowledge.description: Entry through ramp on Nuke requires a flash for trophy room — the most common CT hold position. The entry fragger should push ramp with a pop flash that blinds trophy, then wide swing to clear the angle. Without the flash, trophy kills the entry ~60% of the time.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger on Nuke ramp push to B
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: flash-assisted ramp entry wins ~50% vs ~25% dry against trophy hold

knowledge.title: Nuke AWP: outside AWP from CT heaven
knowledge.description: The CT AWP from heaven (above outside) controls the entire outside area. This is the strongest AWP position on any map — the height advantage makes it nearly impossible to challenge without utility. Hold heaven by default; reposition after one kill.
knowledge.situation: AWP player on CT Nuke, outside defense
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: heaven AWP on Nuke averages 1.3 outside kills per half — highest AWP efficiency in CS2

knowledge.title: Nuke lurk: vent lurk during A execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes A through outside, the lurker drops through vents to B. If the CT rotates from B to A, B site is empty. The vent drop takes 8 seconds — time it so you arrive on B as the CT leaves for the A retake.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Nuke during A execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: vent lurk to B during A execute finds empty B site ~35% of the time on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke support: outside smoke wall for A push
knowledge.description: Support on Nuke T-side throws a 3-smoke wall on outside: smoke heaven, smoke CT red box, smoke garage. This wall blinds all CT outside angles and allows the team to cross safely. Without the smoke wall, crossing outside loses 1-2 players every time.
knowledge.situation: Support player utility for Nuke outside A push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full smoke wall outside reduces crossing deaths from ~40% to ~5%

knowledge.title: Nuke clutch: 1v1 B site — use dark corner
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 on Nuke B as T post-plant, hide in dark corner (back of B behind the pillars). CTs retaking from ramp or vents check the obvious angles first — dark corner is checked last. The surprise factor wins ~55% of 1v1 clutches on Nuke B.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 clutch on Nuke B as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: dark corner post-plant on Nuke B wins ~55% of 1v1 T clutches

knowledge.title: Nuke: vertical awareness — above and below
knowledge.description: Nuke is the only two-level map in CS2. Always be aware of the enemy above or below you. Sound through the floor is muffled — listen for footsteps, nades, and weapon switches. The vertical dimension is Nuke's defining feature; ignore it at your peril.
knowledge.situation: Playing Nuke, ignoring vertical positioning
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: tier-1 teams track vertical enemy positions on Nuke with ~80% accuracy

knowledge.title: Nuke: B retake from ramp with smoke + molly
knowledge.description: CT retaking B on Nuke should smoke the vents exit (cuts T reinforcements) and molly the common post-plant positions. Push from ramp in a 2-3 stack. Without the vent smoke, Ts cycle through vents for free flanking kills.
knowledge.situation: CT retake B site on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ramp retake with vent smoke succeeds ~45% vs ~20% without on Nuke B

knowledge.title: Nuke IGL: outside read for A vs B commit
knowledge.description: At the 0:20 mark, assess outside control. If your outside players face minimal resistance, the CT is stacking inside — commit to A through outside. If outside is heavily contested, the CT is playing outside aggression and B through ramp is soft. Base the A vs B call on outside resistance level.
knowledge.situation: T-side Nuke, IGL reading outside for site commit
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: outside resistance level correctly predicts CT stack distribution in ~62% of rounds on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke support: B vent smoke for retake isolation
knowledge.description: Save one smoke for B retakes. Smoke the vent drop area to isolate T reinforcements dropping from A. Push B from ramp with the smoke active. Without the vent smoke, T players cycle through vents for flanking kills during ~40% of B retakes.
knowledge.situation: Support player holding utility for B retake on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: vent smoke B retake succeeds ~48% vs ~28% without on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke clutch: 1v1 A site — use rafters angle post-plant
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 on Nuke A as T post-plant, hold from rafters behind the metal beam. The rafters angle watches the bomb from elevation and CTs must expose themselves climbing heaven or pushing from CT spawn. The elevation gives you first-shot advantage.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 clutch on Nuke A site as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: rafters angle post-plant on Nuke A wins ~52% of 1v1 T clutches

knowledge.title: Nuke AWP: yard angle from mini
knowledge.description: Hold the outside yard from mini (the small building near outside) with the AWP. Mini gives elevation and cover while watching the entire T outside approach. After the first kill, reposition to heaven — the T will smoke or flash mini on their next push.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP holding outside yard from mini on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mini AWP averages 0.8 outside kills per half on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke anti-strat: counter ramp rush with trophy stack
knowledge.description: If the T keeps rushing ramp, stack two players in trophy room with a molly on ramp entrance and a flash for the counter-peek. The molly delays the rush by 7 seconds and the two-player crossfire from trophy demolishes the disorganized push. This setup shuts down ramp rushes for 3-4 rounds before adaptation.
knowledge.situation: CT losing ramp control to T rushes on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: trophy stack with ramp molly reduces rush success from ~50% to ~18% on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke entry: A from outside with silo flash
knowledge.description: Enter A from outside by throwing a silo flash that bounces off the silo structure and blinds the heaven hold. Push through the outside door immediately after the flash. The silo flash is the highest-value entry flash on Nuke — without it, the heaven player kills entries ~60% of the time.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger pushing A from outside on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: silo flash A entry succeeds ~52% vs ~25% dry on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke lurk: secret lurk during A execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes A from outside, lurk through secret to B. If the CT rotates from B to A, B site is empty. Secret takes 10 seconds — time the drop so you arrive as the CT B player leaves for the A retake. The secret lurk is Nuke's unique vertical flanking play.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Nuke during A execute, secret path
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: secret lurk finds empty B ~32% of the time on Nuke — lower frequency but near-guaranteed plant

knowledge.title: Nuke economy: pistol round ramp stack
knowledge.description: On T pistol, buy P250 + flash and stack 4 ramp + 1 passive outside. The ramp corridor rewards upgraded pistols at close range. Flash the trophy room angle and push through ramp to B. Plant for ramp angle and play post-plant. The ramp pistol stack bypasses the punishing outside sightlines.
knowledge.situation: T pistol round ramp strategy on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ramp stack on T pistol wins ~48% on Nuke — avoids the outside AWP problem

knowledge.title: Nuke positioning: B vents defense from back site
knowledge.description: Hold B from the back-site angle behind the pillars, watching vents. This position covers the vent drop and has an escape route to ramp. The back-site angle forces T vent drops into a kill zone — they land with no cover and 0.5 seconds of landing animation. Never hold directly under vents.
knowledge.situation: CT B defense watching vents on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: back-site vent watch kills vent drops ~65% of the time on Nuke B

knowledge.title: Nuke A retake: mini push with heaven smoke
knowledge.description: Retake A from mini with a smoke on heaven to cut the T post-plant angle. Push from mini toward site with a flash from CT spawn. The mini approach avoids the outside lurk angles. Without the heaven smoke, the T heaven player holds the retake ~50% of the time.
knowledge.situation: CT retaking A from mini on Nuke
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mini retake with heaven smoke succeeds ~48% on Nuke A

knowledge.title: Nuke utility: roof smoke for outside cross
knowledge.description: Throw a roof smoke from T spawn that blocks the heaven and mini sightlines over outside. This single smoke enables safe crossing from T outside to silo. Without it, the CT heaven AWP picks crossers in ~45% of outside pushes. The roof smoke is the most critical T utility on Nuke.
knowledge.situation: T-side Nuke, safe outside crossing
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: roof smoke reduces outside crossing deaths from ~45% to ~8% on Nuke

knowledge.title: Nuke mid-round: vent control after ramp trade
knowledge.description: After winning a ramp trade, take vent control within 5 seconds. Vents connect upper and lower Nuke — controlling vents after ramp gives you a B option without going through outside. Drop one player through vents while the rest hold ramp for the IGL call.
knowledge.situation: T-side Nuke, vent control after ramp trade
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: vent push after ramp trade raises B execute success by ~15 points on Nuke
